text: is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: [0] 这是美国之音慢速英语健康报道。

text: The World Health Organization declared swine flu
zh: [1] 世界卫生组织宣布猪流感

text: as the first international pandemic of the century on June 11th.
zh: [2] 在6月11日成为本世纪首个国际大流行病。

text: The WHO decided to raise its alert against the new H1N1 flu virus
zh: [3] 世卫组织在日内瓦召开专家会议后

text: to the highest level after a meeting of health experts in Geneva.
zh: [4] 决定将针对新型H1N1流感病毒的警戒级别提升至最高等级。

text: The announcement means that countries must keep a close watch on the virus.
zh: [5] 这一宣布意味着各国必须密切监控该病毒。

text: WHO Director General Margaret Chan said,
zh: [6] 世卫组织总干事陈冯富珍表示，

text: The world's rich and poor countries must come together to make sure no countries are left behind
zh: [7] 世界上的富国和穷国必须团结一致，确保没有任何国家被落下

text: without help. As of June 11th, 74
zh: [8] 得不到帮助。截至6月11日，全球74个

text: countries had reported almost 30 ,000 cases of the H1N1 flu virus.
zh: [9] 国家已报告近3万例H1N1流感病例。

text: 144 deaths were reported.
zh: [10] 已报告144例死亡病例。

text: A World Health Organization official, Keiji Fukuda, said
zh: [11] 世界卫生组织官员福田敬二表示，

text: the virus continued to spread in North America.
zh: [12] 该病毒仍在北美持续传播。

text: And there were increasing reports from South America
zh: [13] 南美洲的疫情报告也在增加，

text: as well as from other southern countries.
zh: [14] 其他南半球国家的报告同样如此。

text: The United States has had by far the most confirmed cases.
zh: [15] 美国的确诊病例数目前迄今为止最多。

text: More than 13 ,000 were reported as of June 8th, including 27 deaths.
zh: [16] 截至6月8日，已报告超过13000例，其中27人死亡。

text: The H1N1 outbreak began in Mexico in March.
zh: [17] H1N1疫情于3月在墨西哥爆发。

text: Mexico has reported more than 100 deaths.
zh: [18] 墨西哥已报告超过100例死亡。

text: Dr. Fukuda said the public should understand what a pandemic means.
zh: [19] 福田博士表示，公众应该理解大流行病的含义。

text: It means that the spread of the virus
zh: [20] 这意味着该病毒的传播

text: has continued and that it has become established in at least two areas of the world.
zh: [21] 持续不断，并已在世界上至少两个地区扎根。

text: He said it does not mean that the severity of the situation has increased
zh: [22] 他表示，这并不意味着疫情的严重程度有所加剧，

text: or that people are getting seriously sick at higher numbers.
zh: [23] 也不意味着重症患者的人数在增加。

text: Dr. Fukuda, an acting assistant director general,
zh: [24] 福田博士是代理助理总干事，

text: said the WHO has been working with the member states to prepare for a pandemic.
zh: [25] 他表示，世卫组织一直在与成员国合作，为大流行病做准备。

text: Scientists are working on vaccines to protect against the new virus.
zh: [26] 科学家们正在研制针对这种新病毒的疫苗。

text: Governments have been gathering supplies of antiviral drugs.
zh: [27] 各国政府一直在储备抗病毒药物。

text: But public health officials have also had to consider the risks
zh: [28] 但公共卫生官员也不得不考虑

text: if the public overreacts to a pandemic declaration. Dr. Fukuda pointed out
zh: [29] 公众对大流行病宣布反应过度所带来的风险。福田博士指出，

text: that hospitals might quickly fill with people who are worried but not especially sick.
zh: [30] 医院可能很快就会挤满那些担心却并不算严重生病的人。

text: Then hospitals might not be able to care for other patients who really do need help.
zh: [31] 那样一来，医院可能就无法照顾其他真正需要帮助的病人。

text: The last flu pandemic was in 1968, caused by the so -called Hong Kong flu.
zh: [32] 上一次流感大流行发生在1968年，是由所谓的“香港流感”引起的。

text: Scientists say the common name for the new disease, swine flu, is misleading.
zh: [33] 科学家们说，新疾病的通用名称“猪流感”具有误导性。

text: The virus combines human, bird, and pig viruses.
zh: [34] 该病毒是人类、禽类和猪类病毒的混合体。

text: But eating meat from pigs has not been a danger.
zh: [35] 但食用猪肉并不会带来危险。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: [36] 以上就是美国之音慢速英语健康报道。

text: For more health news, go to voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: [37] 欲了解更多健康新闻，请访问voaspecialenglish.com。
